paper_title: Geochemical Modeling of Mono Lake Evolution

methods.description: Simulate the behavior of various chemical species as freshwater is progressively evaporated using the REACT subprogram of Geochemist's Workbench.

steps.description: Use the REACT subprogram to trace reaction paths of fluids, minerals, and gases, and calculate the distribution of species in aqueous solution, mineral saturation, and gas fugacity.
steps.searchable_summary: simulate chemical species behavior using REACT

methods.method_name: Evaporation and Mixing Model Using PHREEQC
methods.description: Simulate evaporation and mixing of waters to model the geochemical evolution of Mono Lake since the last glacial period using PHREEQC.

steps.description: Use PHREEQC to simulate the geochemical evolution by increasing the proportion of freshwater to initial lake water, maintaining a constant evaporation rate and adding a constant flux of freshwater.
Examine scenarios of freshwater flux changes and their impact on lake chemistry and mineral precipitation.
steps.searchable_summary: analyze impact of freshwater flux changes

methods.description: Develop a Python-based program to automate multiple mixing and evaporation scenarios using PHREEQC.

steps.description: Create a Python program to run multiple mixing and evaporation scenarios until the desired lake volume is achieved.
